zh.lifeSummary: 當Guisla de Lluçà comtessa consort de Barcelona於1011年出生在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿盧c亞時，她的父親Sunifredo II de Llucá36歲，母親Ermesenda de Balsareny111歲。她和Bérenger-Raymond Ier de Barcelone至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1079年在西班牙卡塔倫亞巴薩隆拿享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lluçà
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Adolfo, Antonio, Carmine, Gasper, Giacomo, Mario, Rosario, Sal, Aldo, Antonino, Domenic, Domenico, Dominico, Guido.
Italian and Romanian: from the personal name Luca, from Latin Lucas . Compare Louque .
Italian (southern; Lucà): possibly from a short form of Greek Loukanos, denoting an inhabitant of Lucania (see Lucas ).

variants.name: Lucarelli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Dino, Domenic, Franco, Olindo, Rocci, Rocco, Aldo, Carlo, Caterina, Chiara, Domenico.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a derivative of   Luca  .

variants.name: Luchini
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Oriano, Reno, Aldo, Angelo, Delio, Guido, Livio, Ottavio, Primo, Remo, Romano.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a derivative of   Luca  .

variants.name: Lucas
meanings: English, German, French, Spanish, Portuguese, and Dutch: from the Latin personal name Lucas (Greek Loukas) ‘man from Lucania’. Lucania is a region of southern Italy thought to have been named in ancient times with a word meaning ‘bright’ or ‘shining’ (compare Lucio ). The Christian name owed its enormous popularity throughout Europe in the Middle Ages to Saint Luke the Evangelist, hence the development of this surname and many vernacular derivatives in most of the languages of Europe. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Greek Loukas , Hungarian Lukács (see Lukacs ), German, Dutch, etc. Lukas , Polish Łukasz, Czech and Slovak Lukáš, Czech Lukeš, Slovenian and Croatian Lukež (see Lukes ). Compare Luke .
Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Lùcais (see McLucas ).
History: Jacques Lucas dit Lépine from Port-en-Bessin-Huppain in Calvados, France, married Françoise Capel in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1653.

variants.name: Luchetti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Everardo, Dante, Gino, Italo, Marco, Mario, Olindo, Ottavio, Rocco, Umberto.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a pet form of   Luca  .

variants.name: Louque
meanings: Altered form, under the French influence in LA, of Italian   Luca   ‘Lucas’.

variants.name: Lucca
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Salvatore, Aldo, Angelo, Carlo, Dino, Domenic, Franco, Geno, Giuseppe, Livio, Marco.  Spanish Luis, Ana, Jose, Juan, Julio, Manuel, Orfilia.
Italian:

habitational name from Lucca in Tuscany or from Lucca Sicula in Agrigento province, Sicily, which was called simply Lucca until 1863. It was probably originally named with a Celtic element meaning ‘marshy’.

en.lifeSummary: When Guisla de Lluçà comtessa consort de Barcelona was born in 1011, in Lluçà, Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain, her father, Sunifredo II de Llucá, was 36 and her mother, Ermesenda de Balsareny, was 111. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Bérenger-Raymond Ier de Barcelone. She died in 1079, in Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guisla de Lluçà comtessa consort de Barcelona est née en 1011, à Lluçà, Barcelona, Catalunya, Espagne, son père, Sunifredo II de Llucá, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Ermesenda de Balsareny, avait 111 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Bérenger-Raymond Ier de Barcelone. Elle est décédée en 1079, à Barcelona, Catalunya, Espagne, à l’âge de 68 ans.

Sie hatte mit Bérenger-Raymond Ier de Barcelone mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1079 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Barcelona, Catalunya, Spanien.

ko.lifeSummary: Guisla de Lluçà comtessa consort de Barcelona(이)가 1011년 스페인 카털니아 바셀나 루ca에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sunifredo II de Llucá(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ermesenda de Balsareny(은)는 111세였다. 그녀는 Bérenger-Raymond Ier de Barcelone(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1079년 스페인 카털니아 바셀나에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guisla de Lluçà comtessa consort de Barcelona родилась в 1011 г. в местности ллуca, барселона, каталуня, Испания, её отцу по имени Sunifredo II de Llucá было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Ermesenda de Balsareny, 111.
